---
title: "Clara RANKL RNAseq Analysis"
output:
  html_notebook: default
  pdf_document: default
editor_options:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
---

**Updated by AJB Dec 18 2024**

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook of
Andrew's work on \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

An diagram of the overall RNAseq workflow is shown below. Steps in
yellow were completed prior to this notebook. Steps in light blue are
completed below. Teal squares represent intermediate files generated
throughout the pipeline.

![] (RNASeq_Pipeline.png)

**Required Files For this Notebook**

-   The mapped reads, stored as *.bam* files.
-   The reference genome, converted to *.gtf* format.

### RNAseq Counts and Differential Expression

Begin by installing and loading packages. The RNAseq analysis uses the
package
[Rsubread](https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/vignettes/Rsubread/inst/doc/SubreadUsersGuide.pdf)
to generate mRNA counts for each gene. Then, this pipeline uses
[DESeq2](https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/manuals/DESeq2/man/DESeq2.pdf)
in order to determine differential expression of genes.

```{r}
# IF INSTALLATION IS NECESSARY
# if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
#     install.packages("BiocManager")
# BiocManager::install("Rsubread")
# BiocManager::install("DESeq2")
# BiocManager::install("apeglm")
# BiocManager::install("biomaRt")
# BiocManager::install("PCAtools")
# BiocManager::install("ggforce")

# Load Packages
library(tidyverse)
library(ggtext)
library(glue)
library(ggforce)
library(pheatmap)
library(ggrepel)

library(Rsubread)
library(DESeq2)
library(ashr)
library(ggbeeswarm)
library(biomaRt)
```

Access the aligned reads (SAM format) and run `Rsubread-featureCounts`
to determine how many RNA reads align to each gene.

```{r}
# Generate list of SAM files for featureCounts to use.
align_directory<-"C:/CassatLab/STm_RANKL_RNASeq/02_Mapping/aligned"
files<-list.files(path=align_directory, full.names=TRUE, pattern="\\.bam$")

# Run featureCounts on each file.
counts <- featureCounts(files=files,
                        isGTFAnnotationFile=TRUE,
                        annot.ext="C:/CassatLab/STm_RANKL_RNASeq/GRCm39/Mus_musculus.GRCm39.113.gtf",
                        GTF.featureType="exon",
                        GTF.attrType="gene_id",
                        isPairedEnd=TRUE,
                        genome="C:/CassatLab/STm_RANKL_RNASeq/GRCm39/Mus_musculus.GRCm39.dna_rm.primary_assembly.fa",
                        minFragLength=50,
                        nthreads=8,
                        )
```

Now save the featureCounts object to file. This .txt file will be a
matrix with each gene, and the number of assigned counts from each input
file.

This is a savepoint - and I've transferred this data to the INF drive. I
will not upload the .fastq or .sam files to the INF drive to save \>
20GB of space. As mentioned above the raw data is all available in the
SRA, and my preprocessing pipeline is reproducible in Snakemake.

```{r}
# Write the featureCounts object to a .txt file.
# The lapply function removes weird footers from gene names.
write.table(
  x=data.frame(counts$annotation["GeneID"],
    counts$counts,
    stringsAsFactors=FALSE) %>%
    lapply(gsub, pattern='.t01', replacement='') %>%
    lapply(gsub, pattern='.r01', replacement=''),
  file="counts.txt",
  quote=FALSE,
  sep="\t",
  row.names=FALSE)
```

Read in the counts matrix and load into DESeq2.

```{r}
# Import counts file
cts <- as.matrix(read.table("counts.txt",sep="\t", header=TRUE))
    
# Import column data, saved as tab-delimited .txt, and remove spaces that head some values
coldata <- as.matrix(read.table("seq_reference.txt", sep="\t", header=TRUE)) %>%
  trimws()

# Get order of columns for counts matrix from column data
ctsColOrder <- coldata[,1]
# Append "GeneID" column first
ctsColOrder <- c("GeneID",ctsColOrder)
# Re-order counts matrix 
cts <- cts[,ctsColOrder]

# Extract counts & Ignore first column (geneID) and first row (sample names)
countDataMatrix <- as.matrix(cts[-1,-1])
# Convert to integer
mode(countDataMatrix) <- "integer"
# Set rownames to geneIDs
rownames(countDataMatrix) <- cts[-1,1]
# Set colnames to sample names
colnames(countDataMatrix) <- colnames(cts)[-1]   

dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData=countDataMatrix, 
                              colData=coldata,
                              design = ~condition)
```

Now a brief filtering step to remove rows that have fewer than 10 reads.
```{r}
# Filter rows with less than 10 reads.
smallestGroupSize <- 3
keep <- rowSums(counts(dds) >= 10) >= smallestGroupSize
dds <- dds[keep,]
```

Now we run DESeq to determine differentially-expressed genes between
individual conditions.

```{r}
# Run DESeq2 for each of the comparisons:
dds <- DESeq(dds)
```

## PCA Plots

```{r}
# Get variances from DESeq dataset.
vsd <- vst(dds, blind=FALSE)
# Plot PCA from variances based on condition.
pcaData <- plotPCA(vsd, intgroup="condition", returnData=TRUE)
```

Using the R library PCAtools, we can dig in to the data.

```{r}
library(PCAtools)

vst <- assay(vst(dds,blind=FALSE))

pcaFull <- pca(vst, metadata=colData(dds))
```

4h PCA Plot - v1a

```{r}
# Create dataframe with rotation information (i.e. the PC1/2/3 data to plot)
pcaPlotData <- pcaFull$rotated

# Get percent information
percentVar <- round(pcaFull$variance,2)

# Make PCA Plot with ggplot2
plot <- ggplot()+
  ggforce::geom_mark_ellipse(aes(x=pcaPlotData$PC1,
                                 y=pcaPlotData$PC2,
                                 fill=pcaFull$metadata$condition),
                             lty="blank") +
  geom_point(aes(x=pcaPlotData$PC1,
                 y=pcaPlotData$PC2,
                 fill=pcaFull$metadata$condition),
             size=3, alpha=.7, shape=21,) +

  scale_fill_manual(name = "Condition",
                    values = c(`4h_mock_0`="#048",`4h_mock_100`="#69C",
                               `4h_STm_0`="#970",`4h_STm_100`="#EC6"),
                    breaks = c("4h_mock_0", "4h_mock_100",
                               "4h_STm_0", "4h_STm_100"),
                    labels = c("Mock-0R", "Mock-100R",
                               glue("<i>S.</i>Tm-0R"), 
                               glue("<i>S.</i>Tm-100R"))) +
  scale_shape(labels = c("PBS", "Vancomycin")) +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits=c(-40,40)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits=c(-30,30)) +
  labs(title="PCA Plot - 4h",
       color="Condition",
       shape="Treatment") +
  xlab(paste0("PC1: ",percentVar[1],"% variance")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC2: ",percentVar[2],"% variance")) +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=11, color="black"),
        axis.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size=12)) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size=14, face='bold')) +
  theme(legend.position='right',
        legend.title=element_text(face="bold", size=11),
        legend.text=element_markdown(color = "black", size = 11))

# Show plot
plot
ggsave("4hPCA-1a.png", width = 6, height = 4)
```

4h PCA Plot - v1b

```{r}
# Create dataframe with rotation information (i.e. the PC1/2/3 data to plot)
pcaPlotData <- pcaFull$rotated

# Get percent information
percentVar <- round(pcaFull$variance,2)

# Make PCA Plot with ggplot2
plot <- ggplot()+
  ggforce::geom_mark_ellipse(aes(x=pcaPlotData$PC1,
                                 y=pcaPlotData$PC2,
                                 fill=pcaFull$metadata$condition,
                                 color=pcaFull$metadata$condition)) +
  geom_point(aes(x=pcaPlotData$PC1,
                 y=pcaPlotData$PC2,
                 fill=pcaFull$metadata$condition),
             size=3, alpha=.7, shape=21,) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Condition",
                    values = c(`4h_mock_0`="#048",`4h_mock_100`="#69C",
                               `4h_STm_0`="#970",`4h_STm_100`="#EC6"),
                    breaks = c("4h_mock_0", "4h_mock_100",
                               "4h_STm_0", "4h_STm_100"),
                    labels = c("Mock-0R", "Mock-100R",
                               glue("<i>S.</i>Tm-0R"), 
                               glue("<i>S.</i>Tm-100R"))) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Condition",
                    values = c(`4h_mock_0`="#048",`4h_mock_100`="#69C",
                               `4h_STm_0`="#970",`4h_STm_100`="#EC6"),
                    breaks = c("4h_mock_0", "4h_mock_100",
                               "4h_STm_0", "4h_STm_100"),
                    labels = c("Mock-0R", "Mock-100R",
                               glue("<i>S.</i>Tm-0R"), 
                               glue("<i>S.</i>Tm-100R"))) +
  scale_shape(labels = c("PBS", "Vancomycin")) +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits=c(-40,40)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits=c(-30,30)) +
  labs(title="PCA Plot - 4h",
       color="Condition",
       shape="Treatment") +
  xlab(paste0("PC1: ",percentVar[1],"% variance")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC2: ",percentVar[2],"% variance")) +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=11, color="black"),
        axis.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size=12)) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size=14, face='bold')) +
  theme(legend.position='right',
        legend.title=element_text(face="bold", size=11),
        legend.text=element_markdown(color = "black", size = 11))

# Show plot
plot
ggsave("4hPCA-1b.png", width = 6, height = 4)
```

Now we use a log fold change (LFC) shrinkage correction to improve gene
ranks. This was published in [*Bioinformatics* in
2018.](https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/bty895)

```{r}
# Use the LFC adjustment for each group.
ddsLFC <- lfcShrink(dds, contrast=c("condition", "4h_mock_100", "4h_mock_0"), type="ashr")
```

Check the available comparisons relative to a group, in this case the
mock-infected non-RANKL-treated group at 4h.

```{r}
summary(ddsLFC)
```

We will use the package biomaRt to reference the Ensembl database and
relates the Ensembl gene id (i.e. "ENSMUSG00000064336") to a more
understandable gene name. We will merge this reference with the output
folder to facilitate plotting.

This first block checks biomaRt for all possible attributes available in
the *Mus musculus* database.

```{r}
grcm39 = useEnsembl(biomart='ensembl', dataset="mmusculus_gene_ensembl")

attributeList <- listAttributes(grcm39)
```

Now we will make the reference table with: - Ensembl Gene Name - Gene
Type (i.e. coding, lncRNA, etc) - Gene Name (i.e. "sox9") - Chromosome -
Start Position - Stop Position

It will be saved as "grcm39_ref.txt" for future uses and so we don't
need to access biomaRt the whole time.

```{r}
all_genes <- getBM(attributes=c('ensembl_gene_id','gene_biotype','external_gene_name', 'entrezgene_id','chromosome_name','start_position','end_position'), mart = grcm39)

write.table(all_genes, file="grcm39_ref.txt",
  quote=FALSE,
  sep="\t",
  row.names=FALSE)
```

This block reads in the reference as saved from file.

```{r}
ref <- as.matrix(read.table("grcm39_ref.txt",sep="\t", header=TRUE)) %>%
  trimws()
```

```{r}
# Set locus tags (first column) as row names.
refMatrix <- ref[,-1]
rownames(refMatrix) <- ref[,1]

# Create matrix from results.
resMatrix <- as.matrix(ddsLFC)
# Merge matrix with reference.
resFull <- merge(refMatrix,resMatrix ,by="row.names",all.x=TRUE) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  drop_na()

# Rename "row.names" column to Locus
colnames(resFull)[colnames(resFull)=="Row.names"] <- "locus"

# Save .csv with results.
write.csv(resFull, file="Counts/4h_mock_100_vs_4h_mock_0.csv", row.names=FALSE)
```


## Volcano Plot with Certain Genes Labeled

Read in file and apply significance threshold
Also mark genes to be labeled on plot
```{r}
volcanoData <- read.csv(file="Counts/4h_mock_100_vs_4h_mock_0.csv")

geneLabels <- c("Oscar", "Mmp9", "Acp5", "Ctsk", "Ocstamp", "Tnf", "Il1a")
labelData <- volcanoData |> filter(external_gene_name %in% geneLabels)

```

Make Volcano plot
```{r}
ggplot(resFull, aes(x=log2FoldChange, y=-log10(padj))) + 
  geom_point(size=2, alpha=0.5) +
  geom_point(data=labelData, aes(x=log2FoldChange, y=-log10(padj)),
             size=2, alpha=0.7, color="red") +
  geom_label_repel(data = labelData, aes(label=external_gene_name),
                   min.segment.length=.05,
                   box.padding=0.5,
                   seed=1) +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits=c(-20,20)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,400)) +
  labs(title="Volcano Plot - Mock 0R vs 100R - 4h",
       color="Condition",
       shape="Treatment") +
  xlab("log2FC") +
  ylab("-log10(p.adj)") +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=11, color="black"),
        axis.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size=12)) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size=14, face='bold')) +
  theme(legend.position='right',
        legend.title=element_text(face="bold", size=11),
        legend.text=element_markdown(color = "black", size = 11))
ggsave("4hVolc_Mock0vsMock100-1a.png", width = 6, height = 4)
```

Make MA plot
```{r}
ggplot(resFull, aes(y=log2FoldChange, x=log2(baseMean))) + 
  geom_point(size=2, alpha=0.2) +
  geom_point(data=labelData, aes(y=log2FoldChange, x=log2(baseMean)),
             size=2, alpha=0.7, color="red") +
  geom_label_repel(data = labelData, aes(label=external_gene_name),
                  min.segment.length=.05,
                  box.padding=0.5,
                  seed=1) +
  theme_classic() +
  #scale_x_continuous(limits=c(-20,20)) +
  #scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,400)) +
  labs(title="MA Plot - Mock 0R vs 100R - 4h",
       color="Condition",
       shape="Treatment") +
  xlab("Log2 Mean Expression") +
  ylab("Log2 Fold Change") +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=11, color="black"),
        axis.title=element_text(size=11,face="bold"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size=12)) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size=14, face='bold')) +
  theme(legend.position='right',
        legend.title=element_text(face="bold", size=11),
        legend.text=element_markdown(color = "black", size = 11))
ggsave("4hMA_Mock0vsMock100-1a.png", width = 6, height = 4)
```




## Heatmap using DESeq2 info

```{r}
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData=countDataMatrix, 
                              colData=coldata,
                              design = ~condition)

namedCounts <- counts(dds)
colnames(namedCounts) <- c("4h_mock_0-1", 
                           "4h_STm_0-1", "4h_STm_0-2", "4h_STm_0-3",
                           "4h_STm_100-1", "4h_STm_100-2", "4h_STm_100-3", 
                           "4h_mock_0-2", "4h_mock_0-3",
                           "4h_mock_100-1", "4h_mock_100-2", "4h_mock_100-3"
                       )
write.table(
  x=data.frame(namedCounts),
  file="Counts/named_raw_counts.txt",
  quote=FALSE,
  sep="\t",
  row.names=FALSE)
```

Now a brief filtering step to remove rows that have fewer than 10 reads.

```{r}
# Filter rows with less than 10 reads.
smallestGroupSize <- 3
keep <- rowSums(counts(dds) >= 10) >= smallestGroupSize
dds <- dds[keep,]
```

Normalize raw reads using DESeq2 - a median of ratios normalization.

```{r}
ddsNorm = estimateSizeFactors(dds)
ddsNorm = counts(ddsNorm, normalized = TRUE)
```

But these are the names of the .bam files. Let's change that with an ugly manual list.

```{r}
colnames(ddsNorm) <- c("4h_mock_0-1", 
                       "4h_STm_0-1", "4h_STm_0-2", "4h_STm_0-3",
                       "4h_STm_100-1", "4h_STm_100-2", "4h_STm_100-3", 
                       "4h_mock_0-2", "4h_mock_0-3",
                       "4h_mock_100-1", "4h_mock_100-2", "4h_mock_100-3"
                       )
head(ddsNorm)
```

Now save the normalized counts for each condition:

```{r}
write.table(
  x=data.frame(ddsNorm),
  file="Counts/named_normalized_counts.txt",
  quote=FALSE,
  sep="\t")
```

Now log-transform this data:
```{r}
ddsLog <- log2(ddsNorm+1)
head(ddsLog)
```

Plot the 50 most differentially expressed genes from one of the datasets:

```{r}
# Import DESeq2 Output
plotData <- read.csv("Counts/4h_STm_0_vs_4h_mock_0.csv", header=TRUE)

plotNames <- plotData %>%
    arrange(padj) %>%
    slice_head(n = 50) 

plotNames <- plotNames$locus

ddsLogPlot <- ddsLog %>%
  as.data.frame() %>% 
  filter(mapply(`%in%`,column.names, plotNames))

```


```{r}
pheatmap(ddsLogPlot, 
         cluster_rows = FALSE,                      
         cluster_cols = TRUE,
         show_rownames = TRUE, 
         show_colnames = TRUE,
         treeheight_col = 25,
         treeheight_row = 25,
         scale='none',
         cellheight=10,
         cellwidth=20,
         angle_col=315,
         main = "Log2 Transformed Heatmap",
         filename="Counts/heatmap_log2.png")
```






```{r}
library(pathview)
library(gage)
library(gageData)

data(kegg.sets.mm)
data(sigmet.idx.mm)

kegg.sets.mm = kegg.sets.mm[sigmet.idx.mm]
head(kegg.sets.mm, 3)
```

```{r}
foldChange = resFull$log2FoldChange
names(foldChange) = resFull$entrezgene_id
head(foldChange)
```

Now run gage on the DESeq2 dataset and examine regulation as sets of
KEGG-annotated pathways.

```{r}
keggres = gage(foldChange, gsets=kegg.sets.mm, same.dir=TRUE)
lapply(keggres, head)
```

```{r}
pathview(gene.data=foldChange, species='mmu', pathway.id="04620", out.suffix = "STm4.0-Mock4.0")
```

```{r}
ref <- as.matrix(read.table("grcm39_ref.txt",sep="\t", header=TRUE)) %>%
  trimws()

# Set locus tags (first column) as row names.
refMatrix <- ref[,-1]
rownames(refMatrix) <- ref[,1]

# Setup pathway analysis matrix
keggNames = names(kegg.sets.mm)
KeggPathDF = data.frame(matrix(ncol = 0, nrow = length(keggNames)))
rownames(KeggPathDF) = keggNames

for (condition in c('4h_STm_0', '4h_STm_100', '4h_mock_100', 
                    '24h_STm_0', '24h_STm_100', '24h_mock_0', '24h_mock_100')) {
  
  # Use the LFC adjustment for each group.
  ddsLFC = lfcShrink(dds, contrast=c("condition", condition, "4h_mock_0"), type="ashr")
  
  # Create matrix from results.
  resMatrix = as.matrix(ddsLFC)
  
  # Merge matrix with reference.
  resFull = merge(refMatrix,resMatrix,by=0,all.x=TRUE) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    drop_na()
  
  # Rename "row.names" column to Locus
  colnames(resFull)[colnames(resFull)=="Row.names"] <- "locus"
  
  # Save .csv for individual differential expression results.
  write.csv(resFull, file=paste0(condition,"_vs_4hMock.csv"), row.names=FALSE)
  
  # Pathway analysis
  foldChange = resFull$log2FoldChange
  names(foldChange) = resFull$entrezgene_id
  keggres = gage(foldChange, gsets=kegg.sets.mm, same.dir=TRUE)

  tempKegg = data.frame(keggres$stats)
  colnames(tempKegg) <- c('drop', condition)
  tempKegg <- tempKegg[ , condition, drop = FALSE]

  KeggPathDF <- merge(KeggPathDF, tempKegg, by='row.names',all.x=TRUE)
  KeggPathDF <- data.frame(KeggPathDF, row.names = 1)
  
  #write.csv(KeggPathDF, file="KEGG_4hmock0ref.csv")
}
```

```{r}
# Setup pathway analysis matrix
keggNames = names(kegg.sets.mm)
KeggPathDF = data.frame(matrix(ncol = 0, nrow = length(keggNames)))
rownames(KeggPathDF) = keggNames

tempKegg = data.frame(keggres$stats)
colnames(tempKegg) <- c('drop', condition)
tempKegg <- tempKegg[ , condition, drop = FALSE]

KeggPathDF <- merge(KeggPathDF, tempKegg, by='row.names',all.x=TRUE)
rownames(KeggPathDF) <- KeggPathDF$Row.names
KeggPathDF[,1] <- NULL
```

Now we can preview the summarized data, one by one. These data are
listed in gene order to begin with. These commands also save the
resulting data as *.csv* files for future reference.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}
pcaData <- plotPCA(vsd, intgroup=c("condition", "type"), returnData=TRUE)
percentVar <- round(100 * attr(pcaData, "percentVar"))
ggplot(pcaData, aes(PC1, PC2, color=condition, shape=type)) +
  geom_point(size=3) +
  xlab(paste0("PC1: ",percentVar[1],"% variance")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC2: ",percentVar[2],"% variance")) + 
  coord_fixed()

pcaData
```

### Plotting

Start with a volcano plot to visualize up- and downregulation.

```{r}

SynRefSignif <- transform(SynRef, signif= ifelse(log2FoldChange>=log2(3) | log2FoldChange<=-log2(3) & padj<.001, TRUE, FALSE))

up = length(which(SynRefSignif$signif == TRUE & SynRefSignif$log2FoldChange>=log2(3)))
down = length(which(SynRefSignif$signif == TRUE & SynRefSignif$log2FoldChange<=-log2(3)))

plot <- ggplot(data=SynRefSignif, aes(x=log2FoldChange, y=-log10(padj), color=signif)) +
  geom_point()+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE"="grey", "TRUE"="black")) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(title="Differentially Expressed Genes after Rel-Syn Activation",
       subtitle= paste(up,"Genes Upregulated,",down,"Genes Downregulated"),
       color="Significance")

plot

```
